extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction and category: I counted only the “Against” rows, excluding all “By” rows; criminal proceedings are 0 against the Company, 1 against Directors, 0 against Promoters and 2 against Subsidiaries, while statutory/regulatory proceedings are 2, 4, 2 and 0 respectively, and tax proceedings against the Company and Subsidiaries are 3 and 7 respectively. For the quantified aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries only, I used the table amounts of ₹17.03 million against the Company plus ₹586.21 million against Subsidiaries, excluding promoter/director amounts and all “By” matters, totaling ₹603.24 million. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated Total of ₹570.81 million as of September 30, 2023, not a re-sum of the line items. The company-wide top-five customer concentration is 77.97% for the full Financial Year 2023, measured against total revenue from contracts with customers; no company-wide top-ten percentage is disclosed, and the separate IT/ITES and BFSI percentages are segment-level concentration figures. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10: ₹603.24 million = ₹60.324 crore and ₹570.81 million = ₹57.081 crore.
